info.title: Taskr API
info.description:
## Authentication

This API uses **JWT Bearer** authentication. Every authenticated request must include an `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your_access_token_here>
```

Get an access token by calling `POST /v1/auth/login` with your email and password. Use the returned `refreshToken` to obtain new tokens via `POST /v1/auth/refresh` (rotated on use, with reuse detection).

## File Fields and Uploads

All file fields in the API (such as `avatarUrl`, `coverImageUrl`, `attachments`) expect **URLs** as values, not file objects. To upload a file:

1. **Upload your file** using the file upload endpoint:
   - **Endpoint:** `POST /v1/files`
   - **Authentication:** Requires Bearer token
   - **Content-Type:** `multipart/form-data`
   - **Body:** Form data with `files` field (single or multiple files)
   - **Max size:** 10 MB per file
   - **Allowed types:** `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/gif`, `image/webp`, `application/pdf`

2. **Use the returned URL** in the field of your choice. URLs are validated server-side to ensure they were uploaded through this API.

### Example File Upload Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.taskr.com/v1/files \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -F "files=@/path/to/image.jpg"
```

### Example File Upload Response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Successfully uploaded 1 file(s).",
  "data": {
    "id": "abc123",
    "url": "https://cdn.example.com/uploads/abc123_image.jpg",
    "originalFilename": "image.jpg",
    "fileSize": 12345,
    "fileSizeDisplay": "12.1 KB",
    "contentType": "image/jpeg",
    "createdAt": "2026-07-01T13:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

### URL Validation

When a URL is submitted in a file field (e.g. `coverImageUrl`), the API:
- Verifies the URL format
- Confirms the host is a trusted storage provider (S3, Cloudinary, or local)
- Performs an HTTP `HEAD` request to confirm accessibility
- Validates the `Content-Type` is an allowed image/PDF type
- Rejects files larger than 10 MB

This prevents users from setting arbitrary external URLs as their avatar/cover image.

## Pagination

List endpoints support pagination via query parameters:

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `page` | `1` | Page number (1-indexed) |
| `pageSize` | `20` | Items per page (max 100) |
| `search` | — | Full-text search on the name/title field |
| `startDate` | — | Filter items created on/after this date (ISO 8601) |
| `endDate` | — | Filter items created on/before this date (ISO 8601) |
| `sort` | — | Sort field (e.g. `-createdAt` for descending) |

### Paginated Response Format

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Operation successful",
  "data": [ ... ],
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "pageSize": 20,
    "totalCount": 42,
    "totalPages": 3,
    "hasNext": true,
    "hasPrevious": false
  },
  "errors": null
}
```

| meta field | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `page` | Current page number (1-indexed) |
| `pageSize` | Items per page |
| `totalCount` | Total number of items matching the query |
| `totalPages` | Total number of pages |
| `hasNext` | Whether there is a next page |
| `hasPrevious` | Whether there is a previous page |

## Response Format

All responses use a standard envelope:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Operation successful",
  "data": { ... },
  "errors": null
}
```

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `success` | `true` for success, `false` for errors |
| `message` | Human-readable status message |
| `data` | Response payload. For paginated endpoints this is the items list directly. |
| `meta` | Pagination metadata. Present only on paginated list endpoints. |
| `errors` | Field-level errors (validation) or `null` |

## Status Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `200` | Success |
| `201` | Created |
| `204` | No content |
| `400` | Bad request (malformed JSON, missing required fields) |
| `401` | Unauthorized (missing or invalid token) |
| `403` | Forbidden (insufficient permissions) |
| `404` | Not found |
| `409` | Conflict (duplicate email, etc.) |
| `422` | Unprocessable entity (validation failed) |
| `429` | Too many requests (rate limited) |
| `500` | Internal server error (unhandled exception) |

## Rate Limiting

| Policy | Limit | Applied to |
|---|---|---|
| `auth-strict` | 50 / 5 min per IP | Register, login, refresh, password reset |
| `api-default` | 100 / min per user | All read endpoints (`GET`) |
| `write-strict` | 30 / min per user | All write endpoints (`POST`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`) |

post.summary: Change the password of the currently authenticated user.
post.description:
Requires the existing password for verification. All refresh tokens for
the user are invalidated after a successful change.

post.summary: Authenticate a user and return access + refresh tokens.
post.description:
Exchanges an email and password for a short-lived access token and a
long-lived refresh token. Use the access token in the
`Authorization: Bearer <token>` header on subsequent requests.

get.description: Returns the profile of the user identified by the bearer token.

post.summary: Request a password reset OTP via email.
post.description:
Sends a one-time code to the supplied email if an account exists.
Always returns 200 to avoid leaking which addresses are registered.

post.summary: Confirm a password reset using the OTP.
post.description:
Submits the OTP emailed via `POST /v1/auth/password-reset` along
with a new password. The OTP is single-use.

post.summary: Rotate a refresh token for a new access + refresh pair.
post.description:
Submits a valid refresh token to receive a fresh access token and a
newly rotated refresh token. The previous refresh token is invalidated
(with reuse detection).

Creates a new user with the supplied credentials. Returns the created
user profile. Duplicate email addresses are rejected.

Accepts a `multipart/form-data` body with one or more files in the
`files` field. Each file must be one of the allowed content types
and no larger than 10 MB. The returned URL is what should be stored on
resources (avatar, cover image, task attachments, etc.).

delete.summary: Delete a previously uploaded file by URL.
delete.description:
Removes the underlying object from the configured storage provider.
The URL must be one previously returned by the upload endpoint and
owned by the calling user.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of projects owned by the caller. Supports
search, date filtering, and sorting (see query parameters).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page. Defaults to `20`, max `100`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort expression. Prefix a field name with `-` for descending order (e.g. `-createdAt`).

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional full-text search applied to the primary name/title field of the resource.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Filter results after this date (inclusive). ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Filter results before this date (inclusive). ISO 8601 format.

post.description:
Creates a project owned by the authenticated user. Returns the new
project resource.

get.summary: Get a single project by id.
get.description: Returns the project identified by id if the caller owns it.

patch.description:
Partial update. Only the supplied fields are modified. The caller must
own the project.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the project and cascades to its tasks. Owner only.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of tasks belonging to the project identified
by projectId. Supports status, priority, assignee,
and due-date filtering.

parameters.name: dueDateFrom
parameters.description: Filter tasks due on/after this date (ISO 8601).

parameters.name: dueDateTo
parameters.description: Filter tasks due on/before this date (ISO 8601).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page. Defaults to `20`, max `100`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort expression. Prefix a field name with `-` for descending order (e.g. `-createdAt`).

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional full-text search applied to the primary name/title field of the resource.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Filter results after this date (inclusive). ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Filter results before this date (inclusive). ISO 8601 format.

post.summary: Create a task in a project.
post.description:
Creates a new task under the project identified by
projectId. The caller must own the project.

get.description: Returns the task identified by id if the caller owns its project.

patch.description:
Partial update. Only the supplied fields are modified. Moving a task
to status `Done` sets `completedAt`; moving it out clears it.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the task. Project owner only.

accessToken.description: Short-lived JWT used in the `Authorization` header.

refreshToken.description: Long-lived token used to obtain new access tokens via the refresh endpoint.

accessTokenExpiresAt.description: Timestamp (UTC) at which the access token expires.

refreshTokenExpiresAt.description: Timestamp (UTC) at which the refresh token expires.

AuthTokensResponse.description: Access + refresh token pair returned by login and refresh endpoints.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

AuthTokensResponseApiResponse.description:
Standard success response envelope. Every successful API response is
wrapped in this shape so the client always sees the same top-level
structure.

oldPassword.description: The user's current password, required for verification.

newPassword.description: The new password to set on the account.

name.description: Display name of the project. Required.

description.description: Optional longer description shown on the project page.

coverImageUrl.description: Optional cover image URL. Must be uploaded via `POST /v1/files`.

metadata.description: Optional free-form key/value metadata stored on the project.

title.description: Short title describing the task. Required.

description.description: Optional longer description or notes for the task.

attachments.description: Optional list of attachment URLs uploaded via `POST /v1/files`.

assigneeId.description: Optional user id to assign the task to. Must be a known user.

metadata.description: Optional free-form key/value metadata stored on the task.

id.description: Server-generated identifier for the file record.

url.description: Publicly accessible URL where the file can be fetched. Store this on the owning resource.

originalFilename.description: The filename provided by the client at upload time.

contentType.description: MIME type of the uploaded file.

FileResponse.description: Response for a single uploaded file.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

FileResponseApiResponse.description:
Standard success response envelope. Every successful API response is
wrapped in this shape so the client always sees the same top-level
structure.

password.description: Account password in plaintext (sent over TLS).

data.description: Response payload. For paginated responses this is the items list directly.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

ObjectApiResponse.description:
Standard success response envelope. Every successful API response is
wrapped in this shape so the client always sees the same top-level
structure.

email.description: Email address of the account being recovered.

otp.description: 6-digit one-time code sent to the user's email.

newPassword.description: The new password to set on the account.

email.description: Email address of the account to recover. Must exist.

description.description: Longer description of the project, if any.

ownerId.description: Identifier of the user who owns the project.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp (UTC) the project was last updated.

metadata.description: Free-form key/value metadata attached to the project.

ProjectResponse.description: Project resource returned by project endpoints.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

ProjectResponseApiResponse.description:
Standard success response envelope. Every successful API response is
wrapped in this shape so the client always sees the same top-level
structure.

data.description: Response payload. For paginated responses this is the items list directly.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

ProjectResponsePagedApiResponse.description: Success response envelope for paginated list endpoints.

refreshToken.description: The refresh token previously issued by login or a prior refresh call.

email.description: Unique email address. Used as the login identifier.

password.description: Account password. Stored as a bcrypt hash server-side.

firstName.description: Given name shown in the UI.

lastName.description: Family name shown in the UI.

avatarUrl.description: Optional avatar image URL. Must be uploaded via `POST /v1/files`.

metadata.description: Optional free-form key/value metadata stored on the user record.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

RegisterResponseApiResponse.description:
Standard success response envelope. Every successful API response is
wrapped in this shape so the client always sees the same top-level
structure.

TaskPriority.description: Priority level of a task. Used for sorting and filtering.

projectId.description: Identifier of the project the task belongs to.

projectName.description: Display name of the project the task belongs to.

assigneeId.description: Identifier of the assigned user, if any.

assigneeName.description: Display name of the assigned user, if any.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp (UTC) the task was last updated.

completedAt.description: Timestamp (UTC) the task was moved to `Done`, if ever.

metadata.description: Free-form key/value metadata attached to the task.

TaskResponse.description: Task resource returned by task endpoints.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

TaskResponseApiResponse.description:
Standard success response envelope. Every successful API response is
wrapped in this shape so the client always sees the same top-level
structure.

data.description: Response payload. For paginated responses this is the items list directly.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

TaskResponsePagedApiResponse.description: Success response envelope for paginated list endpoints.

name.description: New project name. Omit to leave unchanged.

description.description: New description. Pass an empty string to clear.

coverImageUrl.description: New cover image URL. Pass an empty string to clear.

metadata.description: Replacement metadata. Pass an empty object to clear.

UpdateProjectRequest.description: Payload for `PATCH /v1/projects/{id}`. All fields are optional.

description.description: New description. Pass an empty string to clear.

attachments.description: Replacement attachment URL list. Pass an empty list to clear.

dueDate.description: New due date. Pass null to clear.

assigneeId.description: New assignee. Pass null to unassign.

metadata.description: Replacement metadata. Pass an empty object to clear.

UpdateTaskRequest.description: Payload for `PATCH /v1/tasks/{id}`. All fields are optional.

createdAt.description: Timestamp (UTC) the user record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp (UTC) the user record was last updated.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp (UTC) of the user's most recent successful login, if any.

metadata.description: Free-form key/value metadata attached to the user record.

UserResponse.description: Public-facing user profile returned by auth and user endpoints.

errors.description: Field-level errors, or `null` for success responses.

UserResponseApiResponse.description:
Standard success response envelope. Every successful API response is
wrapped in this shape so the client always sees the same top-level
structure.

Bearer.description: JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Account creation, login, refresh, and password recovery.

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Project lifecycle: list, create, read, update, and delete.

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: Task lifecycle scoped to a project: list, create, read, update, delete.

tags.name: Files
tags.description: Multipart file upload and deletion for user-uploaded assets.
